title: cassia antillana
intro: Cassia antillana, also known as West Indian senna, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is native to the tropical regions of the West Indies. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a laxative and anti-inflammatory agent due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides and saponins. The health benefits of Cassia antillana include the relief of constipation, reduction of fever, and treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Cassia antillana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
